Class TransferController

java.lang.Object
com.bank.core.infrastructure.in.web.TransferController
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/v1/transfers") @Validated public class TransferController extends Object
Inbound REST adapter for internal fund transfer operations.

Exposes POST /api/v1/transfers, which requires an Idempotency-Key HTTP header. The key guarantees that submitting the same request multiple times within the 24-hour TTL window produces exactly one transfer.

Idempotent replays return the original transaction result with HTTP 200 (not 201), allowing clients to distinguish a new transfer from a replayed one by checking the X-Idempotent-Replay response header set by this controller.

Since:
1.0.0
Version:
1.0.0
Author:
Core Banking Team

  • Method Details

    • transfer

      @PostMapping @ResponseStatus(CREATED) public TransactionResponse transfer(@RequestHeader("Idempotency-Key") @NotBlank(message="Idempotency-Key header is required") @Size(max=255,message="Idempotency-Key must not exceed 255 characters") @NotBlank(message="Idempotency-Key header is required") @Size(max=255,message="Idempotency-Key must not exceed 255 characters") String idempotencyKey, @Valid @RequestBody @Valid TransferRequest request)
      Initiates an internal fund transfer between two accounts.

      The Idempotency-Key header must be supplied with every request. A client-generated UUID v4 is the recommended format. If the key has been seen before within its TTL window, the cached transaction is returned.

      Parameters:
      idempotencyKey - the caller-supplied unique key for this request
      request - the transfer payload
      Returns:
      the resulting transaction with HTTP 201
